title: Time-domain observation of interlayer exciton formation and thermalization in a MoSe2/WSe2 heterostructure
type1_summary: Most Figshare TXT/DAT files are clean, directly plottable source/processed data tables for spectra, transient absorption maps, and time traces. These are consistent with the paper’s explicit deposit of processed spectroscopic data.
type2_summary: The simulation-like ksnapshots file is best treated as raw/intermediate theoretical output requiring interpretation, and the large PDF files are supplementary documentation rather than clean data tables.
classification_notes: The dataset is dominated by cleaned, figure-ready spectroscopy tables from Figshare, consistent with Type 1. The PDFs are supplementary documents, not data tables. The ksnapshots file is the main ambiguous case; it is tabular but appears to be a simulation snapshot or intermediate theoretical output, so it was conservatively assigned Type 2.

file_classifications.file_evidence: Large PDF (18.3 MB) from nature_source_data; no tabular structure or machine-readable data content is indicated.
file_classifications.reasoning: This is not a replot-ready data table and does not appear to be raw instrument output. As a supplementary PDF, it is best treated as documentation/supporting material rather than Type 1 or Type 2 data.
file_classifications.ambiguity: The PDF could contain embedded source-data pages or figures, but without page-level inspection it cannot be safely classified as data.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: PDF document; no tabular columns reported

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies interlayer exciton formation in a MoSe2/WSe2 van der Waals heterostructure using ultrafast broadband transient absorption spectroscopy at 77 K, combined with microscopic exciton-phonon theory. The key finding is that interlayer charge transfer occurs on a ~100 fs timescale, but the optically bright interlayer exciton signal rises much more slowly (~1 ps) because hot interlayer excitons must thermalize through phonon-assisted scattering before contributing strongly to the optical response.
measurement_types: control transient absorption on isolated monolayers and blank substrate

figures.description: Schematic type-II band alignment of the MoSe2/WSe2 heterostructure showing intralayer and interlayer exciton transitions.
Cartoon of interlayer exciton formation: resonant excitation in MoSe2 followed by interlayer hole transfer to WSe2.
77 K linear absorption and photoluminescence spectra of the heterostructure, showing intralayer exciton peaks and a lower-energy interlayer exciton emission peak.
2D transient absorption map (ΔT/T) versus pump-probe delay and probe photon energy, showing positive photobleaching and negative photoinduced absorption features.
Transient absorption spectra at selected delays, with the near-IR interlayer exciton region magnified to reveal the weak ILX peak.
Time traces of the MoSe2 A exciton, WSe2 A exciton, and interlayer exciton transient signals with fitted rise times.
Cartoon and model of excitation and scattering pathways: intralayer excitation, intervalley scattering, and phonon-assisted interlayer hole tunneling leading to interlayer exciton populations.
Calculated differential transmission time traces for AMo, AW, and ILX excitons compared with the pump pulse.
Decomposition of the calculated AMo signal into contributions from coherent polarization, intralayer populations, and interlayer populations.
Decomposition of the calculated AW signal showing contributions from interlayer populations.
Decomposition of the calculated ILX signal showing delayed rise from hot interlayer exciton thermalization.
Schematic of hot interlayer exciton relaxation and thermalization through optical and acoustic phonon scattering.
Calculated momentum-dependent interlayer exciton populations at different delays overlaid with Pauli-blocking weights for AW and ILX transitions.
Polarization-resolved second harmonic generation used to determine twist angles of the heterostructures.
Pump/probe spectral characterization and transient absorption setup schematic.
Photoluminescence of individual monolayers and heterostructure comparison showing quenching of intralayer PL.
Fluence-dependent transient absorption showing persistence of ILX photobleaching and emergence of negative PIA at higher excitation density.
Comparison of calculated and measured AMo dynamics for isolated monolayer and heterostructure.
Measurements on a near anti-aligned (57°) heterostructure.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw optical measurements including photoluminescence spectra, linear absorption spectra, transient absorption transmission spectra (dispersed broadband probe detected by spectrometer or monochromator/photodiode), and polarization-resolved second harmonic generation patterns used to determine twist angle. The transient absorption experiments were performed in transmission geometry at 77 K with pump-probe delay scans and broadband probe spectra.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The main transient absorption figures are processed ΔT/T maps, extracted spectra at selected delays, and fitted time traces with rise-time estimates. The theoretical figures are calculated differential transmission traces and momentum-resolved population distributions derived from microscopic Bloch-equation simulations.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states: 'The processed spectroscopic data of the manuscript are available at https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.24428992. Supplementary information data will be made available by request to the corresponding authors.' It also states that code pertaining to the theoretical model will be made available by request to the corresponding authors.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw measurements are described in the Methods as optical spectra and time-resolved transmission data acquired with two TA setups, a fast silicon spectrometer at 1 kHz and a monochromator/photodiode lock-in setup at 100 kHz, plus PL and SHG measurements. However, the paper does not state that raw files are deposited publicly.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include ΔT/T maps, spectra at selected delays, time traces at specific probe energies, fitted rise times, and calculated theoretical differential transmission curves. The deposited Figshare item is explicitly described as 'processed spectroscopic data.'
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Yes. The data availability statement implies figure-related processed spectroscopic data are provided on Figshare, i.e. source-data-like material for the manuscript figures. Supplementary information data are available by request, suggesting additional figure-specific datasets may exist but are not publicly linked in the text.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis notebook or executable script is deposited in the text. Theoretical-model code is only said to be available by request to the corresponding authors, so scripts are not publicly provided in the paper text.
dataset_characterization.data_organization: figure-based processed dataset on Figshare; supplementary data by request; raw data organization not specified
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper explicitly provides processed spectroscopic data on Figshare, which should be sufficient to replot the main experimental figures. Raw measurement files are not described as publicly available, and code is not openly deposited in the text.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset semantics are strongly centered on processed spectroscopy rather than raw instrument output. The paper distinguishes experimental raw acquisition in Methods from the publicly available processed spectroscopic data. Theoretical results are derived from a microscopic model and are not raw measurements. The text does not specify file formats, folder structure, or whether the Figshare deposit contains per-figure source data tables, but it is likely figure-oriented processed data.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: Methods describe direct acquisition of PL, absorption, SHG, and TA spectra/time traces with specific instruments and geometries, indicating raw measurements were collected.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: Data availability explicitly says 'processed spectroscopic data' are available on Figshare, which is consistent with source data for figures.
classification_prior.both_evidence: The paper contains both raw measurement acquisition descriptions and an explicit public deposit of processed spectroscopic data.
data_availability_section_relevant: The processed spectroscopic data of the manuscript are available at https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.24428992.
Supplementary information data will be made available by request to the corresponding authors.
Code pertaining to the theoretical model in this work will be made available by request to the corresponding authors.
classification_prior.review_notes: Do not classify the Figshare deposit as raw data; the paper explicitly calls it processed spectroscopic data. Theoretical curves are derived, not measured. Supplementary data are not clearly public and are only promised by request, so their exact contents remain unknown.
